tldr: Anthropic's always-on agent platform Conway leaks via TestingCatalog, revealing a sidebar-based persistent agent with webhooks, Chrome integration, and a custom extension system
Anthropic registers AnthroPAC with the FEC on April 3 as AI industry midterm spending tops $300 million, amid an ongoing Pentagon lawsuit over a 'supply chain risk' designation
Apple pulls the $100M-valued vibe coding app Anything from the App Store, then restores it days later, as AI-generated submissions drive an 84% spike in quarterly app reviews
Microsoft ships Copilot Researcher Critique and Council modes where GPT generates reports and Claude reviews them, outperforming Perplexity Deep Research by 13.8% on accuracy benchmarks

topics.title: Anthropic's Always-On Agent Platform 'Conway' Leaks, Revealing Persistent Sidebar With Webhooks and Extensions
topics.summary: TestingCatalog published exclusive details on Conway, an internal Anthropic project that turns Claude into a persistent, always-on agent environment. The system features a standalone sidebar with search, chat, and system panels, webhook triggers for external services, Chrome browser integration, and a custom extension framework using .cnw.zip packages.
key_takeaways: Conway runs as a standalone sidebar with three sections: Search, Chat, and System, separate from the standard Claude interface
Public webhook URLs let external services wake Conway instances, enabling event-driven automation without keeping a session open
An Extensions area supports custom tools, UI tabs, and context handlers via .cnw.zip packages, suggesting an app-store-like ecosystem
Claude in Chrome can connect directly to Conway instances, bridging browser context with the persistent agent environment
topics.article:
On April 1, TestingCatalog published an exclusive look at Conway, an internal Anthropic project that could reshape how Claude operates day to day. Instead of launching Claude for individual conversations, Conway positions the model as a persistent agent that lives in a sidebar and stays active across sessions.

The leaked interface reveals three core sections: Search (with experimental hotkeys), Chat (the primary conversation view), and System (configuration). But the real signal is in the infrastructure. Conway supports public webhook URLs that let external services trigger the agent, meaning it can respond to events from tools like GitHub, Slack, or custom APIs without a human opening a tab. Combined with Chrome browser connectivity, Conway could browse, research, and act on information autonomously.

The extension system is particularly telling. An Extensions area lets users install custom tools, UI tabs, and context handlers through .cnw.zip packages. References to an "Epitaxy UI" suggest a separate operator interface for managing Conway instances. This architecture points toward an ecosystem where third-party developers build specialized capabilities on top of a persistent Claude agent.

For creative directors and builders, Conway represents the shift from "AI tool you open" to "AI agent that runs." If shipped, it would compete directly with OpenAI's super app vision while offering something different: an always-on agent layer that integrates into your existing browser and tool stack rather than replacing them.

topics.title: Anthropic Launches AnthroPAC as AI Industry Pours $300M Into Midterms, Pentagon Lawsuit Escalates
topics.summary: Anthropic filed FEC paperwork on April 3 to establish AnthroPAC, an employee-funded political action committee backing candidates from both parties who engage with AI policy. The move follows a $20 million donation to Public First Action in February and comes amid a federal lawsuit against the Pentagon after Defense Secretary Hegseth labeled Anthropic a 'supply chain risk.'
key_takeaways: AnthroPAC (FEC ID C00946111) is an employee-funded PAC with contributions capped at $5,000 per year, overseen by a bipartisan board
AI industry midterm spending has topped $300 million, exceeding the crypto sector's 2024 levels
Anthropic is suing the Pentagon after being designated a 'supply chain risk,' with a federal judge blocking punitive actions against the company
Competing PACs include Leading the Future ($125M from OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman), while Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta run similar employee PACs
topics.article:
Anthropic filed paperwork with the Federal Election Commission on April 3 to establish AnthroPAC, the company's first political action committee. The employee-funded PAC permits individual contributions up to $5,000 per year and will back current and emerging congressional candidates from both parties who demonstrate engagement with AI policy.

This is not Anthropic's first political move. In February, the company donated $20 million to Public First Action, a bipartisan 501(c)(4) focused on AI education and federal governance. Public First has since raised $75 million total backing pro-regulation candidates. The broader AI industry has collectively poured more than $300 million into 2026 midterm races, exceeding the crypto sector's spending in 2024.

The timing is loaded. Anthropic is locked in a federal lawsuit against the Department of Defense after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth designated the company a "supply chain risk" in March. The Pentagon paused or canceled contracts including a reported $200 million opportunity, reportedly in response to Anthropic's restrictions on Claude's use in fully autonomous lethal weapons and mass surveillance. A federal judge issued a temporary block on punitive actions, framing the designation as potential First Amendment retaliation.

For anyone watching AI's intersection with government, the stakes are becoming clearer. Anthropic is simultaneously fighting for federal contracts, funding political candidates, and drawing ethical lines on military applications. AnthroPAC joins Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta's existing employee PACs, while competing against Leading the Future, a $125 million fund backed by OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman.

topics.title: Apple Pulls $100M Vibe Coding App From App Store as AI Submissions Surge 84%
topics.summary: Apple removed the AI-powered app builder Anything from the App Store on March 26, citing guideline 2.5.2 against apps executing unreviewed code. The app, valued at $100 million after an $11 million raise, was restored on April 3 after modifications. Vibe coding tools drove an 84% jump in quarterly App Store submissions, with review times ballooning from 24-48 hours to 7-30+ days.
key_takeaways: Anything was pulled March 26 and restored April 3, after Apple cited guideline 2.5.2 prohibiting apps from executing code that changes functionality after review
Vibe coding drove an 84% quarterly jump in App Store submissions, with 557,000 new apps in 2025 (the most since 2016)
Review times ballooned from 24-48 hours to 7-30+ days at peak, straining Apple's review infrastructure
Vibe coding platforms are booming: Cursor at $2B+ revenue, Lovable at $200M revenue, Replit targeting $1B for 2026
topics.article:
Apple's escalating enforcement against vibe coding apps reached a peak in late March when it pulled Anything, a $100 million-valued app that lets users build software through natural language prompts, from the App Store entirely. The removal, citing guideline 2.5.2 against apps downloading or executing code that changes their functionality, came after months of tension.

The timeline tells the story. Anything launched on iOS without issues in November 2025. By December, Apple began blocking updates. On March 18, Apple started removing vibe coding apps broadly, targeting Replit and Vibecode among others. Co-founder Dhruv Amin tried to comply by updating the app to preview vibe-coded apps in a web browser rather than within the app itself. Apple rejected the workaround and pulled Anything on March 26. The app returned to the App Store on April 3 after further modifications.

The underlying numbers explain Apple's anxiety. Vibe coding drove an 84% jump in quarterly App Store submissions, pushing full-year 2025 to 557,000 new apps, the highest since 2016. December alone saw a 56% year-on-year spike in iOS app launches. Review times ballooned from 24-48 hours to 7-30+ days, straining the infrastructure that underpins Apple's quality control model.

Apple insists it is "not targeting vibe coding as a category" but enforcing existing guidelines. The distinction matters: educational coding apps are permitted if source code remains "completely viewable and editable by the user." The line Apple is drawing is between tools that help users learn to code versus platforms that generate and execute code dynamically, bypassing the review process that keeps the App Store curated.

topics.title: Meta Rolls Out End-to-End AI Ad Campaigns: Give It a URL and Budget, AI Does the Rest
topics.summary: Meta's Advantage+ platform now handles full campaign automation: provide a business URL and budget, and AI generates creatives, targets audiences, optimizes placement, and manages bids. Sixty-five percent of advertisers have adopted Advantage+, seeing up to 32% CPA reduction. A new Image-to-Video tool converts product photos into multi-scene video ads.
key_takeaways: End-to-end automation means advertisers provide only a URL and budget; AI handles creative generation, targeting, placement, and bidding
65% of advertisers now scale campaigns through Advantage+, with campaign consolidation delivering up to 32% CPA reduction
New Image-to-Video tool converts up to 20 product images into polished multi-scene video ads for Facebook and Instagram
Meta's AI video ad tools have reached a $10 billion annualized revenue run-rate
topics.article:
Meta's advertising platform has crossed a threshold that every creative team should understand. Advantage+ now offers end-to-end AI-automated campaigns where an advertiser provides nothing more than a business URL and a budget. The AI handles everything else: creative generation, audience targeting, placement optimization, and bid management.

The adoption numbers confirm this is not experimental. Sixty-five percent of advertisers are now scaling campaigns through Advantage+, and those who migrated from fragmented campaign structures saw up to 32% reduction in cost per acquisition. Meta's AI video ad tools have hit a $10 billion annualized revenue run-rate, signaling that automated creative is not just working but working at scale.

The latest addition is an Image-to-Video tool that converts up to 20 product images into polished multi-scene video ads optimized for Facebook and Instagram placements. Combined with AI dubbing, AI-generated music, and persona-based image generation, Meta is building a creative production pipeline inside the ad platform itself.

For creative directors and agencies, the strategic question is shifting. The competitive advantage no longer comes from manual campaign optimization or even media buying expertise. It comes from strategic input quality: the brief, the brand positioning, the first-party data you feed the system, and the creative asset diversity you provide as raw material. The humans who understand brand strategy and creative direction become more valuable as the execution layer automates. The humans who only knew how to push buttons in Ads Manager have a problem.

topics.title: Microsoft Ships Copilot Researcher With Dual-Model Critique: GPT Generates, Claude Reviews
topics.summary: Microsoft's Copilot Researcher now features Critique mode where one AI model generates reports and another independently reviews them for accuracy, plus a Council mode where Claude and GPT each produce full reports and a third AI compares them. Internal benchmarks show Critique outperforms Perplexity Deep Research by 13.8% on accuracy.
key_takeaways: Critique mode uses one model for generation and a second for independent review of accuracy, completeness, and source quality
Council mode has Claude and GPT each produce full reports, then a third AI highlights agreement, divergence, and unique insights
DRACO benchmark testing shows Critique outperformed Perplexity Deep Research by 13.8%, with the largest gains in analysis depth (+3.33 points)
Available through Microsoft's Frontier program for Enterprise, with some Business plan accounts already seeing Council options
topics.article:
Microsoft introduced a genuinely novel pattern for AI accuracy with its upgraded Copilot Researcher: instead of trusting a single model's output, it uses multiple AI models that check each other's work.

Critique mode is the more practical of the two new features. One AI model generates the research report while a second independently reviews it, checking source reliability, whether the report fully addresses the request, and whether claims are supported by citations. Microsoft plans to make this the default when "Auto" is selected in the model picker. The reviewing model acts as a quality gate, catching hallucinations and gaps before the report reaches the user.

Council mode goes further. Both an Anthropic Claude model and an OpenAI GPT model independently produce complete reports on the same topic. A third AI then compares the two, highlighting where they agree, where they diverge, and what unique insights each brings. This gives users a multi-perspective view rather than a single model's interpretation of a topic.

The benchmark results are compelling. Testing against the DRACO benchmark, which measures research accuracy, completeness, and objectivity across 100 complex tasks in 10 fields, Critique scored 13.8% higher than Perplexity Deep Research. The largest improvement was in breadth and depth of analysis (+3.33 points), followed by presentation quality (+3.04) and factual accuracy (+2.58).

For anyone producing research-heavy work, this multi-model pattern is worth watching. The idea that AI outputs should be reviewed by a different AI before delivery is a practical answer to the trust problem that has held back enterprise adoption.

topics.title: Webflow Ships AI-Powered AEO Tools to Optimize Sites for LLM Discovery
topics.summary: Webflow rolled out Answer Engine Optimization features that automatically optimize site content for LLM consumption alongside traditional SEO. The AI-powered audit panel checks for missing schema markup, meta descriptions, and alt text across entire sites, then uses AI to generate contextually relevant content for those elements.
key_takeaways: AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) tools automatically format site content for LLM consumption alongside traditional search engines
Site-wide audit panel checks all pages and assets for missing alt text, meta titles, descriptions, and schema markup in a single pass
Webflow AI generates contextually relevant metadata, alt text, and schema markup, with human review before publishing
Early adopters reported 330+ new citations and a 24% lift in impressions from FAQ automation and content refreshes
topics.article:
Webflow quietly shipped a set of tools that address a problem most websites have not even started thinking about: how your content appears when an LLM summarizes it instead of when a human reads it.

The new AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) capabilities sit alongside Webflow's existing SEO tools and focus on making your site discoverable by AI systems like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini when they answer user questions. The core feature is an audit panel that scans your entire site for missing elements that affect both SEO and AEO: image alt text, page titles, meta descriptions, and schema markup. It runs site-wide and page-level checks, catching issues across asset libraries, static pages, and CMS collection pages.

The AI assist layer is where it gets practical. Webflow AI can generate contextually relevant alt text, meta titles and descriptions, and schema markup across your site. For CMS-heavy sites, it can automatically define metadata from CMS fields. Everything goes through human review before publishing, keeping you in control of what represents your brand to both search engines and AI models.

Early adopters using Webflow's FAQ automation and content refreshes reported 330+ new citations and a 24% lift in impressions. Enterprise customers get additional governance capabilities to control AI feature usage on individual sites.

For creative directors managing client websites, AEO is becoming a deliverable, not a nice-to-have. As AI-powered search gains share from traditional Google results, the sites that are structured for LLM consumption will capture the citations and referrals that replace traditional blue links.
